page: Amazon Web Services c6gd.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by custom AWS Graviton processors utilizing the ARM64 architecture. It features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs running at 2.5 GHz on the Neoverse-N1 microarchitecture, with 32.0 GB of system memory providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio. The instance is built on the AWS Nitro System hypervisor and includes 950 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage alongside a 5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.

Performance benchmarks place this instance in the average tier for most multi-core and database operations. It achieves a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 24294.54 and a PassMark CPU score of 9808.46. Memory bandwidth is notable, reaching 946.78 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, though large-block uncached writes drop to 67.98 GB/sec. Single-core performance is relatively weaker, as evidenced by a Geekbench single-core score of 934.0 and a weak rating in single-threaded bzip3 decompression.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM-based architecture provides a resource-dense profile for multi-threaded workloads, balancing compute power with high-speed local NVMe storage. However, cryptographic and single-threaded tasks show lower efficiency. This instance is suited for workloads such as multi-threaded database operations, static web serving, software compilation, and distributed data processing where local high-speed scratch storage is required.
description: Amazon Web Services c6gd.4xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance powered by 16 dedicated AWS Graviton cores running at 2.5 GHz. It features 32.0 GB of system memory, 950 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and a 5 Gbps baseline network interface. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers balanced multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 24294.54 and a PassMark database score of 10377.61. While cached memory read bandwidth is high at 946.78 GB/sec, single-core performance and cryptographic operations are weaker. This hardware profile is efficient for multi-threaded workloads, software compilation, static web serving, and database applications requiring fast local scratch space.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6gd.4xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance with 16 dedicated Graviton cores, 32 GB RAM, and 950 GB local NVMe SSD storage, delivering solid multi-core and database performance.

tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated cores and high-speed local NVMe storage for balanced multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated ARM64 Graviton cores running at 2.5 GHz
32 GB system memory and 950 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth powered by the Nitro hypervisor
Strong multi-threaded performance and high cached memory bandwidth
Suited for software compilation, database operations, and web serving
